title: Desarrollo Humano Internacional
content: Desarrollo Humano Internacional acts as a critical technical and educational communication bridge that transforms meteorological information into a survival tool for civil society.
snippet: Desarrollo Humano Internacional acts as a critical technical and educational communication bridge that transforms meteorological information into a survival tool for civil society.

title: AI in disaster risk reduction: Who is being left out?
content: Artificial intelligence (AI) is becoming part of disaster risk reduction (DRR). It is already being used to forecast hazards , map exposure and guide decisions about where assistance may be needed.
snippet: Artificial intelligence (AI) is becoming part of disaster risk reduction (DRR). It is already being used to forecast hazards , map exposure and guide decisions about where assistance may be needed.

title: Special accreditation applications for UN Diplomatic Conference on Protection of Persons in the Event of Disasters (PPED)
content: The United Nations General Assembly has launched negotiations on a new international convention on the Protection of Persons in the Event of Disasters (PPED).
snippet: The United Nations General Assembly has launched negotiations on a new international convention on the Protection of Persons in the Event of Disasters (PPED).

title: Hidden warm water beneath Antarctica could rapidly raise global sea levels
content: Hidden warm-water traps beneath Antarctica may be melting the continent’s ice far faster than scientists realized.
snippet: Hidden warm-water traps beneath Antarctica may be melting the continent’s ice far faster than scientists realized.

title: Eastern Africa is splitting apart, but not where we expected
content: The Turkana Rift Zone in Kenya entered a critical stage in continental breakup about 4 million years ago.
snippet: The Turkana Rift Zone in Kenya entered a critical stage in continental breakup about 4 million years ago.

title: Visualizing climate extremes: Climate Jellyfish for Italy, the new high-resolution tool by CMCC
content: A new high-resolution visualization tool developed by CMCC reveals not only how Italy is warming, but also how climate variability and extremes are evolving across the country.
snippet: A new high-resolution visualization tool developed by CMCC reveals not only how Italy is warming, but also how climate variability and extremes are evolving across the country.

title: Low snow in Eurasia linked to wildfires in California
content: Scientists found that low autumn snow levels in western Eurasia are associated with dry, warm winters in California, increasing the Golden State’s wildfire risk.
snippet: Scientists found that low autumn snow levels in western Eurasia are associated with dry, warm winters in California, increasing the Golden State’s wildfire risk.

title: Medieval Japanese poetry and buried trees help elucidate volatile space weather
content: Combining carbon-14 measurements taken at the highest level of precision with historical literature lays a new foundation for studying unpredictable and hazardous solar proton events.
snippet: Combining carbon-14 measurements taken at the highest level of precision with historical literature lays a new foundation for studying unpredictable and hazardous solar proton events.

title: 2026 Global report on internal displacement
content: The Global Report on Internal Displacement 2026, published by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC), examines displacement trends across 146 countries and territories affected by conflict, violence and disasters.
snippet: The Global Report on Internal Displacement 2026, published by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC), examines displacement trends across 146 countries and territories affected by conflict

title: Genome sequencing is rewriting the history of disease outbreaks – but without social context, it can tell only part of the story
content: Genome sequencing has changed how disease detectives study outbreaks by allowing them to read a pathogen’s genes as a biological record of where it came from and how it spread.
snippet: Genome sequencing has changed how disease detectives study outbreaks by allowing them to read a pathogen’s genes as a biological record of where it came from and how it spread.

title: Forest fires in Western Himalayas moving to higher altitudes, warns CSIR-IHBT study
content: The region records nearly 1,300 forest fire incidents annually on average.
snippet: The region records nearly 1,300 forest fire incidents annually on average.

title: AI closes data gaps and shows how extreme weather emerges on Earth
content: Researchers from the ETH have now introduced a new artificial intelligence (AI) model that has learned these interactions and feedback autonomously and, compared to previous AI models, more precisely captures how air, land and water interact on Earth.

title: Preparing for the next pandemic, operationalizing global health security: from preparedness to action

title: Comprehensive hazard assessment of tropical cyclone-induced wind, rainfall, and storm surge: A case study of Zhejiang Province, China
content: In this study, the coastal counties of Zhejiang Province were selected as a case study to assess the hazard of tropical cyclones by comprehensively considering the wind, rainfall, and storm surge.
snippet: In this study, the coastal counties of Zhejiang Province were selected as a case study to assess the hazard of tropical cyclones by comprehensively considering the wind, rainfall, and storm surge.

content: This briefing paper examines the role of social protection systems in responding to irreversible climate-induced loss and damage.
snippet: This briefing paper examines the role of social protection systems in responding to irreversible climate-induced loss and damage.

title: How extreme temperatures and humidity raise health risks
content: Extreme temperatures, both hot and cold, can lead to increased mortality, and there are many examples of such events.
snippet: Extreme temperatures, both hot and cold, can lead to increased mortality, and there are many examples of such events.

title: El Niño likely to return: the case for early action
content: Climate models are converging: El Niño is likely to return by mid-2026 and could be strong.
snippet: Climate models are converging: El Niño is likely to return by mid-2026 and could be strong.

title: Ministry of Education and MDMC strengthen school preparedness through training of disaster safe and psychosocial education units in schools

title: Thirty years of earthquake retrofit in the city of Los Angeles: Trends, drivers, and implications for seismic resilience
content: This study examines three decades of seismic retrofit activity in the City of Los Angeles to assess how the implementation has varied across space, time, and socially vulnerable communities.
snippet: This study examines three decades of seismic retrofit activity in the City of Los Angeles to assess how the implementation has varied across space, time, and socially vulnerable communities.

title: Cyclone Gabrielle exposed the risks of forestry slash. New research suggests little has changed
content: When Cyclone Gabrielle tore through New Zealand’s in 2023, it left behind more than silt and floodwaters. Rivers were choked with forestry debris, beaches littered with logs, and homes, bridges and farmland buried under tonnes of forestry slash

title: Hantavirus: the ‘silent’ virus
content: As a hantavirus outbreak makes global headlines, CSIRO researchers are investigating how these rodent-borne viruses spread and why different strains target specific human organs, like the lungs or kidneys, with such varying levels of severity.

title: After disasters: stories from Talisay

title: Hantavirus: A cruise ship, a deer mouse and the fictional line between human and animal health
content: In February 2025, the classical pianist Betsy Arakawa died. Her husband, the actor Gene Hackman, died a week later of heart disease. The pathogen that killed her was hantavirus, almost certainly picked up from deer mouse droppings on the property.

title: Climate-induced internal migration for domestic work in India: Gendered risks and policy responses

snippet: This issue brief examines how climate change is reshaping internal migration patterns in India, particularly among women and adolescent girls entering informal domestic work in urban centres.

snippet: This work presents a climate change-informed modeling framework for assessing the inoperability of interdependent critical infrastructures exposed to NHs.

title: Turning weather and climate data into early health action
content: Early warning is most effective when governance, data, and protocols align, helping health systems act earlier on climate-sensitive risks.
snippet: Early warning is most effective when governance, data, and protocols align, helping health systems act earlier on climate-sensitive risks.

title: Study finds wetter storms mean drier conditions over time
content: The world’s rainfall is increasingly packed into bigger storms and longer dry spells.
snippet: The world’s rainfall is increasingly packed into bigger storms and longer dry spells.

content: A new probabilistic model shows how climate change affects typhoon variance
snippet: A new probabilistic model shows how climate change affects typhoon variance

snippet: In coastal flooding areas like Virginia Key and Key West, the Florida Current has a significant impact on sea level variability.

title: Volcanoes may have trapped Earth in a 56-million-year ice age
content: The volcanoes may have helped trigger the Sturtian ice age by removing huge amounts of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
snippet: The volcanoes may have helped trigger the Sturtian ice age by removing huge amounts of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.

title: Weather data inequity in a flash flood hotspot
content: By improving their weather data, bolstering emergency response, and sharing resources, counties in Southwest Virginia can efficiently prepare for future floods.
snippet: By improving their weather data, bolstering emergency response, and sharing resources, counties in Southwest Virginia can efficiently prepare for future floods.

title: As El Niño approaches, scientists predict fierce heatwaves, wildfires and floods
content: Emerging Pacific Ocean heat, combined with ongoing human-caused global warming, is a grim recipe for deadly climate extremes. Heat alone already kills more than 500,000 annually.
snippet: Emerging Pacific Ocean heat, combined with ongoing human-caused global warming, is a grim recipe for deadly climate extremes. Heat alone already kills more than 500,000 annually.

title: Surges of concentrated precipitation can lead to dryer landscapes
content: Snow and rain in the American West is concentrating at one of the highest rates in the world, researchers found, with implications for ecosystems, water management and this year’s El Niño.
snippet: Snow and rain in the American West is concentrating at one of the highest rates in the world, researchers found, with implications for ecosystems, water management and this year’s El Niño.

title: Even quiet hurricane seasons can roar

title: After the storm passes, inequities worsen

title: Leptospirosis in Europe: climate change will raise the risk
content: Warming temperatures and changing precipitation patterns are projected to increase incidence, expand spatial distribution and extend the transmission season.
snippet: Warming temperatures and changing precipitation patterns are projected to increase incidence, expand spatial distribution and extend the transmission season.
